options: An Rh-positive mother carries an Rh-negative fetus
An Rh-negative mother carries an Rh-positive fetus
Both mother and fetus are Rh-negative
s2.explain: Rh incompatibility occurs when an Rh-negative mother carries an Rh-positive fetus, and maternal exposure to Rh-positive RBCs causes sensitization.

s3.q: Fetal red blood cells enter the maternal circulation mainly at:
s3.explain: Fetomaternal hemorrhage occurs mainly at delivery when the placenta separates, or during invasive procedures/abdominal trauma.

s3.q: Maternal anti-D antibodies are of which immunoglobulin class and cross the placenta?
s3.explain: IgG antibodies cross the placenta and cause fetal hemolysis. Initial sensitization produces IgM, but subsequent exposure produces IgG.

s4.q: Assessment of severity in Rh-sensitized pregnancy includes all EXCEPT:
options: Middle cerebral artery peak systolic velocity (MCA-PSV) by Doppler
s4.explain: Assessment: maternal antibody titers, MCA-PSV Doppler, amniotic fluid delta OD 450 (Liley curve), and fetal ultrasound for hydrops.

s5.q: Prophylaxis for Rh incompatibility includes anti-D immunoglobulin given:
options: At 28-32 weeks and after delivery if baby is Rh-positive
s5.explain: Anti-D is given at 28-32 weeks antenatally and within 72 hours of delivery if baby is Rh-positive, plus after any sensitizing event.

s8.q: Exchange transfusion in the Rh-affected neonate is performed to:
options: Remove bilirubin and anti-D antibodies, and correct anemia
s8.explain: Exchange transfusion removes bilirubin, anti-D antibodies, and sensitized RBCs while correcting anemia with compatible Rh-negative blood.

s9.q: ABO incompatibility differs from Rh incompatibility in that:
options: ABO is more severe and often causes hydrops
ABO often affects the first pregnancy and is typically milder
ABO only affects mothers with blood type AB
s9.explain: ABO incompatibility often affects the first pregnancy (mild) as anti-A/anti-B are naturally occurring IgM that don't cross placenta well. It is typically milder than Rh disease.
